steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank sits between “instrument” (the final word of the subject noun phrase) and “are” (the main verb of the independent clause). It marks the boundary between subject and verb.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A subject and its main verb must not be separated by punctuation in standard English. The correct answer is B—”instrument” connects directly to “are” without any punctuation. All three alternatives violate this rule by inserting a mark (colon, dash, or comma) at the subject-verb boundary. That matches choice B — B is correct.
